nodes.name: auto_merge_worktree.sh
nodes.summary: Automated worktree merge loop that calls merge_worktree.sh repeatedly and invokes the Claude CLI to diagnose and fix quality-gate failures or git conflicts, retrying up to MAX_RETRIES times before giving up.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates AI-assisted repair to the 'claude -p' CLI, passing structured failure context via a mktemp prompt file to avoid quoting issues.

nodes.name: create_worktree.sh
nodes.summary: Creates a named git worktree on a 'wt/<slug>' branch from the current HEAD of main, installs a fresh virtual environment inside the worktree, and writes a merge-authorization lock file to prevent premature merges.
nodes.languageNotes: Commits any dirty main state before branching (WIP snapshot) to ensure the worktree's base commit is clean; mirrors the main venv using pip install -e '.[dev]'.

nodes.name: merge_worktree.sh
nodes.summary: Squash-merges a completed feature worktree into main after verifying the merge-auth lock is absent; refreshes the main venv if pyproject.toml changed, runs the quality gate with coverage, commits, pushes, and cleans up the worktree and branch.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a precise exit-code protocol (0-5) to distinguish authorization blocks, git conflicts, gate failures, preflight errors, and infrastructure failures, enabling the auto_merge_worktree.sh retry loop to respond appropriately.

nodes.name: quality_gate.sh
nodes.summary: The single sanctioned quality gate for the project: runs strict pyright type-checking, isort + black formatting, a second pyright pass post-format, and pytest (parallel by default via pytest-xdist); optionally measures coverage and enforces a one-way ratchet against coverage_baseline.txt.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinguishes exit code 1 (genuine check failure — fix the code) from exit code 2 (infrastructure/environment failure — stop and ask a human) so callers can automate retry logic without masking environment breakage.

nodes.name: infra_error
nodes.summary: Prints an environment-problem message to stderr and exits with exit code 2 (EXIT_INFRA), signalling to callers that the failure is not a code problem and must be resolved by a human.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints the script's header comment block as usage documentation by stripping the leading comment markers with awk.

nodes.name: header
nodes.summary: Prints a formatted section banner to stdout, used to visually delimit each gate step (pyright, isort, black, pytest, coverage) in the terminal output.
